headline: Ugandan Coffee Farmers Overcome Drought Through Innovation
meta_description: Ugandan coffee farmers are using regenerative agriculture methods to defy severe drought and maintain their harvests in a changing climate.
summary_short: Ugandan coffee farmers are using regenerative agriculture techniques to overcome drought and sustain their livelihoods despite climate challenges.
body:
Coffee farming in Uganda faces mounting pressure from shifting rainfall patterns and prolonged dry seasons, yet farmers in the region are discovering that regenerative agricultural practices offer genuine resilience. By working with the soil rather than against it—building organic matter, managing water retention, and rotating crops—they are maintaining productive farms even when drought would otherwise devastate yields.

These methods go beyond mere survival; they improve soil health and water-holding capacity over time, creating systems more robust than conventional monoculture. As climate variability becomes more pronounced, the success of these Ugandan farmers offers a practical blueprint for communities worldwide. Their work shows that adaptation to a changing climate need not mean abandoning farming—it means farming in a way that listens to and works with natural cycles.
